skill.name: pexoai-agent
skill.description: Use this skill when the user wants to produce a short video (5–120 seconds). Supports any video type: product ads, TikTok/Instagram/YouTube content, brand videos, explainers, social clips. USE FOR: video production, AI video, make a video, product video, brand video, promotional clip, explainer video, short video.
skill.summary: AI video creation agent that handles scriptwriting, shot composition, transitions, and music for short videos
skill.author: pexoai

security_audit.summary: This is a legitimate AI video generation CLI tool. All 492 static findings are legitimate tooling patterns: shell commands for CLI operations, network access to pexo.ai API only, filesystem access for config storage, and API key access for authentication. The skill implements proper security practices including API key masking in diagnostics and temporary file isolation via mktemp.

medium_findings.title: Shell Command Execution Patterns
medium_findings.description: Shell command substitution and backtick patterns detected across 316 locations in scripts and documentation. These are legitimate CLI tooling patterns for invoking pexo-chat.sh, pexo-project-create.sh, and other scripts. No user input injection vectors identified.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern detection flags shell scripting as suspicious, but these are standard CLI tool implementations with no command injection risks

medium_findings.title: Network Access to External API
medium_findings.description: Network requests to external URLs detected. All network activity is confined to pexo.ai API endpoints only, verified by code inspection of _common.sh curl calls.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network access is required for API communication and is restricted to the legitimate pexo.ai service

medium_findings.title: Environment Variable Access for Configuration
medium_findings.description: Access to PEXO_API_KEY and PEXO_BASE_URL environment variables. These are required for API authentication. The pexo-doctor.sh script implements proper API key masking via the mask_secret function.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Environment access is standard practice for API-based CLI tools; credential handling includes masking for security

low_findings.title: Filesystem Access for Configuration Storage
low_findings.description: Access to ~/.pexo/config for configuration and ~/.pexo/tmp for temporary files. Standard CLI tool practice using mktemp for secure temp file creation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard filesystem operations for configuration and temp files; no sensitive data exposure

low_findings.title: Documentation References to Shell Commands
low_findings.description: Documentation files contain shell command examples for troubleshooting. These are instructional references, not executable code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Documentation of shell commands for user reference; no execution risk

dangerous_patterns.title: Heuristic: Code Execution + Network + Credential Access Combination
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analyzer flagged combination of external commands, network access, and credential access. This is expected for any API-based CLI tool and represents legitimate functionality for video generation via the Pexo API.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: All three capabilities are required for the skill to function; no evidence of malicious exfiltration or unauthorized actions

content.user_title: Create professional short videos with AI
content.value_statement: Creating marketing videos requires expensive equipment and video production skills. This skill connects you to an AI agent that handles scriptwriting, shot composition, and video production for short clips.
actual_capabilities: Create short videos (5-120 seconds) via AI-powered production agent
Upload images, videos, and audio files as reference materials
Receive AI-generated videos with automatic scriptwriting and composition
Request revisions and iterate on video concepts
Polling workflow with real-time status updates
limitations: Requires PEXO_API_KEY for authentication with pexo.ai service
Video generation takes 15-20 minutes for short clips
Cannot process web URLs directly; must download and upload files first
Only supports specific file formats: images (jpg, png, webp), videos (mp4, mov, avi), audio (mp3, wav, aac)

use_cases.title: Marketing team creates product videos
use_cases.description: Marketing teams use this to quickly generate product demonstration videos for social media campaigns without hiring video editors.

use_cases.title: Content creator produces TikTok content
use_cases.description: Content creators generate short-form vertical videos with AI assistance for platforms like TikTok and Instagram Reels.

use_cases.title: Developer automates video asset creation
use_cases.description: Developers integrate AI video generation into workflows for creating product demo videos and tutorial content.

prompt_templates.title: Basic video request
prompt_templates.prompt: I want a product video for [product description]
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when user wants a simple video without uploaded files

prompt_templates.title: Video with reference image
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a promotional video using this product image: upload the image first, then request a video
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when user provides a reference image to incorporate

prompt_templates.title: Revision request
prompt_templates.prompt: Make the video more [specific style: energetic, professional, playful] and focus on [specific feature]
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when user wants to refine an existing video

prompt_templates.title: Multi-video campaign
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a series of 3 short videos for a [campaign theme] across [platforms]
prompt_templates.scenario: Use for larger content production workflows

output_examples.input: I want a product video for my running shoes
output_examples.output: Video generation started. Project created at https://pexo.ai/project/proj_xxx. Estimated completion: 15-20 minutes. You will receive a download link when ready.

output_examples.input: Make it more energetic with close-up shots
output_examples.output: Revision submitted to Pexo. Project updated at https://pexo.ai/project/proj_xxx. Polling for new video...

best_practices: Upload reference images or videos when available to improve AI accuracy
Keep initial requests simple; let the AI agent ask clarifying questions
Use the project link to monitor progress during the 15-20 minute generation window
anti_patterns: Do not add technical parameters like duration or aspect ratio unless the user specifies them
Do not combine multiple requests into one message; send separate requests
Do not send asset IDs without wrapping them in proper XML tags

faq.question: How long does video generation take?
faq.answer: Short videos (5-15 seconds) take approximately 15-20 minutes. Longer or more complex videos may take longer.

faq.question: What file formats are supported?
faq.answer: Images: jpg, png, webp, bmp, tiff, heic. Videos: mp4, mov, avi. Audio: mp3, wav, aac, m4a, ogg, flac.

faq.question: Can I use web URLs as references?
faq.answer: No. You must download web files first, then upload them using the pexo-upload.sh workflow.

faq.question: What is the maximum video duration?
faq.answer: Videos can be 5-120 seconds. The AI agent may ask about duration preferences during production.

faq.question: How do I get an API key?
faq.answer: Sign up at pexo.ai to get your PEXO_API_KEY. Configure it in ~/.pexo/config or as an environment variable.

faq.question: Can I request revisions?
faq.answer: Yes. After receiving a video, provide feedback and the AI agent will generate a new version using the same project.
